channel.title: Tale: Site-Wide Behavioral Control
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Tale: Site-Wide Behavioral Control" - The twist at the end is that the Foundation is being morally ambiguous. You'll never see it coming.

item.title: Re: Tale: Site-Wide Behavioral Control
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the speedy reply, it's a great help! I'll notify you when it's posted.

item.title: Re: Tale: Site-Wide Behavioral Control
item.content:encoded:
Let's get this show on the road, shall we?

+ Line-by-Line
– hide block

fields which emit fluctuating radio waves within controlled parameters

"emitting fluctuating radio waves …" would flow better. Nothing major, your grammar is solid in any case.

She was a researcher, not a soldier

I know you're italicizing for emphasis, but I don't feel that that's the place to do it.

twenty-nine (29)

As far as I know, people don't really do this anymore.

which syphon energy

While syphon is technically correct, siphon is the more recognizable and used form.

control of jet.

Just a tone thing, "control of the aircraft" would be better.

– hide block
+ Conceptual/Other
– hide block

This is a really ambitious concept that you've built up, and it's definitely well thought out. As far as your plot being clear, it took me a little bit to get through all the size-0 font, but I got through it. However, it took me a bit of time to actually get to the meat of the tale because of all the wading through the talk about how the the antimemetic systems work.

– hide block

All said, you've got a very well-polished piece that I would wager is close to completion. However, I would definitely get another set of eyes on it from someone who's a little more well-versed in the more meta side of the fiction of the site than I.

item.title: Tale: Site-Wide Behavioral Control
item.content:encoded:
Hey, all. I wrote a longer tale about a system Site-17 has that is ostensibly there to make sure the staff are immune to memetic entities. It's another step in my connected universe between all my tales and SCPs.

A thing to note is that the tale follows a terminal layout, so in the final version, the pages shown in the sandbox would be real new webpages, reached by clicking the links.

Here's the link to the sandbox, and a few things I'm interested in:

  • Abuse and misuse of the words "anti-memetic", "memetic", "cognitohazardous" and "infohazardous".
  • The actual story of the tale not being clear enough.
  • The hidden messages in the Memetic Dissemination Process page; they're offset by blue periods, but I don't know if those are noticeable enough or if they fit with the flow of the story. I understand it can break immersion to copy and paste 0-point font.
  • The introduction of the concept of Ultraphysics, which was part of my SCP-4260. It's essentially the study of the embodiments of concepts. I'm worried that someone going in without reading 4260 will be confused.
